tldr: SK Hynix made a record-breaking debut on Nasdaq, raising $26.5 billion.
Deadly wildfires in southern Spain have killed at least 12 people and left 23 unaccounted for.
Tensions between the US and Iran persist with renewed fighting over the Strait of Hormuz, although talks are expected to continue.

market.why: The S&P 500 and Nasdaq Composite closed higher, primarily driven by strong performance in the tech sector. SK Hynix, a chip giant, saw its shares surge in a bumper US listing, raising $26.5 billion. Additionally, optimism surrounding Meta Platforms' AI strategy contributed to market gains, and stablecoin issuer Circle received approval to operate as a trust bank.

yield_10y.why: The provided articles do not offer a reason for the movement in the 10-year Treasury yield.

vix.why: The provided articles do not offer a reason for the movement in the VIX.

tech.summary: SK Hynix, a major memory chip supplier, made a significant Nasdaq debut, raising $26.5 billion amid high demand driven by the AI boom.
SpaceX is planning to launch an additional 100,000 Starlink satellites, aiming to increase bandwidth by a factor of 100.
Sunrun is initiating a pilot program that offers to pay customers to host AI compute units in their homes, exploring a new model for distributed AI data centers.

world.summary: At least 12 people have died and 23 are missing due to a fast-spreading wildfire in southern Spain, exacerbated by heat, strong winds, and dry vegetation.
Russian attacks in Ukraine have resulted in four deaths, including a teenager, while Ukrainian forces continue to target Russian oil infrastructure.
US and Iranian forces have exchanged attacks over the Strait of Hormuz in recent days, though President Trump indicated that the US would continue negotiations with Iran.
